LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - GRANTED. 1)PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSITION BRIEF IS DEFECTIVE AS IT LACKS A STATEMENT OF UNDISPUTED FACTS. CCP SECTION 473C(B); 2)THE INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS CLAIM IS DENIED BECAUSE PLAINTIFF DID NOT COMPLY WITH THE PRE-FILING REQUIREMENT (GOVERNMENT CODE SECTION 911.2) AND DID NOT FILE A WRITTEN APPLICATION FOR LEAVE TO PRESENT A LATE CLAIM (GOVERNMENT CODE SECTION 911.4); 3)ASSUMING A PRIMA FACIE CASE OF AGE DISCRIMINATION, DEFENDANT HAS PROFFERED AMPLE EVIDENCE OF THE HIRING PROCESS AND THE REASONS FOR NOT RE-HIRING PLAINTIFF. PLAINTIFF DID NOT PROVIDE SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE OF DISCRIMINATORY ANIMUS OR PRE-TEXT; 4)PLAINTIFF DID NOT OFFER ANY EVIDENCE IN SUPPORT OF HIS CLAIM FOR INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS; DEFENDANT PROVIDED EVIDENCE SHOWING THE REASONABLENESS OF ITS CONDUCT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: JOHN K. STEWART, REPORTER: SUSAN CROZIER, CSR #4761
ORDER GRANTING DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

THERE WAS NO APPEARANCE BY EITHER SIDE FOR THE SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE IN DEPT. 613. ORDER GRANTING SUMMARY JUDGMENT MOTION FILED OCT. 23, 2007. THE SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE WAS ORDERED OFF CALENDAR. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, REPORTER: NOT REPORTED, CLERK: DAVID V. SWANSON, DEPT. 613
